title: ECMAScript - Wikipedia
body: ECMAScript is the standard for the JavaScript language, intended to ensure the interoperability of web pages across different web browsers.

title: JavaScript | MDN
body: JavaScript (JS) is a lightweight interpreted (or just-in-time compiled) programming language with first-class functions. While it is most well-known as the scripting language for Web pages, many non-browser environments also use it, such as Node.js, Apache CouchDB and Adobe Acrobat.

title: New TOEFL (2026): Writing Sections - TOEFL Preps
body: New TOEFL Writing (Jan 21, 2026-). Overall Structure.The key to success in this part is knowing how to structure an email effectively as well as maintaining the required level of formality. You can practice email-writing questions below.

title: Cursor: The best coding agent
